issue.title: Record oracle response for original-path PHP emission strategy

artifacts.role: oracle response summary, escalation criteria, gates, and follow-up task list
amended PHP emission architecture naming WPHX PHP as the original-path adapter emitter
amended snapshot policy for WPHX-generated public shells
operations documentation for the hybrid stock-Haxe-PHP plus WPHX-public-adapter strategy
program progress rollup and next compiler-pressure gates
root agent rule clarifying stock Haxe PHP versus WPHX public adapter responsibility
operations agent instruction mirror for the same compiler boundary

claims: The oracle response recommends continuing the hybrid strategy for the next Core slices.
Stock Haxe PHP is recorded as the private implementation emitter, not as sufficient public WordPress file emission by itself.
WPHX PHP is recorded as the bounded WordPress original-path public adapter emitter.
No Haxe PHP generator fork or broad reflaxe.php target is justified yet.
Future target compatibility is preserved by treating Haxe-owned semantics and typed adapter contracts as the durable assets rather than generated PHP text.
non_claims: This receipt does not implement the adapter IR ADR, bootstrap ADR, shell snapshot lane, processHeaders generation, buildCookieHeader generation, conditional declarations, include side effects, or shell retirement policy.
This receipt does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership.
This receipt does not prove stock Haxe PHP is sufficient for modern WordPress public PHP files.
